G.Ramona is more situational than her OG. OG Ramona's Exalt always gives you a 0 cost card of your choice, and because it's tied to Exalt, you can activate it every turn. G.Ramona has a similar effect tied but it's on one of her basic cards, so you have to rng to pull it, it'll only reduce the cost by 1, *and* it's limited to cards in your draw pile. Her gimmick is having a triggering bonus effect every 3-ish turns (Think of it like Ultra teams' Leap mechanic?) so you get the full "card of your choice 0 cost" every 3 turns. 

Queen's Sword is actually pretty decent for stacking Power too when you don't have a built up roster, and she's quite self-sufficient? G.Ramona's dmg takes a bit longer to scale up and prefers having extra support on the side to boot

At 1st level, you can magically summon a spectral tentacle that strikes at your foes. As a bonus action, you create a 10-foot-long tentacle at a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The tentacle lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature to create another tentacle.

When you create the tentacle, you can make a melee spell attack against one creature within 10 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 cold damage, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. When you reach 10th level in this class, the damage increases to 2d8.

As a bonus action on your turn, you can move the tentacle up to 30 feet and repeat the attack.

You can summon the tentacle a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
